comments.author: @ATOMInference
comments.text: This explains a lot of what we're seeing on the pricing side. Agentic workloads don't just consume more tokens, they consume them differently. A single agent task can fan out into 50,000 tokens across multi-step reasoning, tool calls and verification loops. That is a 1,000x

comments.author: @KislayParashar1
comments.text: Makes sense. Agents do a lot of coordination, memory handling, and I/O, not just raw compute. GPUs run the model, but CPUs end up managing everything around it.
